tagline: Configure the recovery period for dropped managed tables.
what_it_does: This update allows users to configure the recovery period for dropped Unity Catalog managed tables, enabling a range from 0 hours (immediate purge) to 30 days. By default, the recovery period is set to 7 days, during which users can recover dropped tables using the UNDROP TABLE command. This feature can be configured at both the catalog and schema levels, with schema-level settings taking precedence.
why_it_matters: Configuring the recovery period enhances data governance by providing flexibility in data retention policies, allowing organizations to balance between data recovery needs and cloud storage costs. This is particularly beneficial for businesses that frequently create and drop tables, as it can lead to significant cost savings while ensuring critical data is protected against accidental deletions.
plain_summary: This update allows users to configure the recovery period for dropped Unity Catalog managed tables, enabling a range from 0 hours (immediate purge) to 30 days. By default, the recovery period is set to 7 days, during which users can recover dropped tables…

capabilities.title: Configurable Recovery Period
capabilities.desc: Set the recovery period for dropped tables between 0 to 30 days.

capabilities.title: Immediate Purge Option
capabilities.desc: Option to disable recovery by setting the period to 0 hours.

capabilities.title: Schema-Level Configuration
capabilities.desc: Recovery settings can be applied at both catalog and schema levels.

capabilities.title: Accidental Drop Protection
capabilities.desc: Provides a safeguard against accidental deletions of critical data.

prerequisites: Manage privilege or ownership on the catalog or schema
use_cases: Organizations needing flexible data retention policies
Teams frequently creating and dropping tables for testing or development
Data governance frameworks requiring protection against accidental data loss
limitations: Only applies to tables dropped after the configuration is set
Does not affect tables that were already dropped

steps.title: Access Unity Catalog
steps.desc: Navigate to the Unity Catalog in your Databricks workspace.

steps.title: Select Catalog or Schema
steps.desc: Choose the catalog or schema for which you want to configure the recovery period.

steps.title: Set Recovery Period
steps.desc: Configure the recovery period to your desired duration (0-30 days).

steps.title: Save Configuration
steps.desc: Apply and save the changes to enforce the new recovery settings.

steps.title: Verify Settings
steps.desc: Check the configuration to ensure it reflects the desired recovery period.

key_takeaway: The ability to configure the recovery period for dropped managed tables enhances data governance and cost management in Databricks.
